facts.shortSummary: Leaving the council, the party reviews the evacuation plan for Karawa, compares priorities, and decides to spend the afternoon preparing for Raven's Hold.
facts.longSummary: The session opens in the uneasy aftermath of the evacuation council as rumors spread through Karawa and Havdar starts rallying anyone who can ride and fight. The party talks through the plan to send most of the town west to Tokra while Candrosa and the Shakun mystai fortify the temple, Alesh scouts south, and the group itself heads north to Raven's Hold. Their recap also turns toward the lightning storm, the battered frontier villages, and what they still need to learn before they leave.

location.context: the oasis and town center immediately after the evacuation council
location.notes: The party remains in town, orienting themselves after the meeting and deciding how to use the afternoon.

npcs.name: Havdar
npcs.notes: The party understands that he is rallying fighters and will escort the refugees west.

npcs.name: Alesh
npcs.notes: She is discussed as the scout riding south while the party heads north.

npcs.name: Candrosa
npcs.notes: The plan assumes he and the mystai will stay behind and hold the temple.

organizations.name: Shakun Mystai
organizations.notes: The order is expected to stay in Karawa and fortify the temple while the rest of the town flees.

facts.shortSummary: The party spends the afternoon chasing local knowledge, learning more about Raven's Hold, the Red Mesa, and the divine forces at work around Karawa.
facts.longSummary: With the broad plan set, the group scatters across Karawa to gather what local knowledge they can before the northern expedition. Wellby presses the Charmhearts for more about Raven's Hold and the route there, Seeker studies the strange age and layered stone of the Red Mesa, and Kenzo questions Candrosa about the north, Aagir, and old Dunmari shrines. By the end of the beat, the party has a clearer picture of Raven's Hold as an old border fort and trade post, but not the certainty they were hoping for.

location.context: the Charmheart camp, the Red Mesa, and the Temple of Shakun
location.notes: The beat covers several same-town errands without leaving Karawa.

npcs.name: Candrosa
npcs.notes: He shares limited historical knowledge of Raven's Hold and explains more about Aagir and the northern frontier.

npcs.name: Callie Charmheart
npcs.context: halfling traveler familiar with the road north
npcs.notes: She helps Wellby picture Raven's Hold and the route Ander took when he wandered into danger.

npcs.name: Aagir
npcs.context: god associated with frontier soldiers and destruction
npcs.notes: Candrosa suggests that older shrines in the north would most likely have been dedicated to Aagir.

organizations.name: Shakun Mystai
organizations.notes: Candrosa's answers and Seeker's mesa observations keep the temple and its guardians central to the conversation.

facts.shortSummary: As fear deepens in Karawa, the party gathers supplies, plans a rendezvous, and says farewell to Alesh before she rides south.
facts.longSummary: The rest of the day becomes practical preparation. Delwath scrounges potions and other supplies while Seeker starts thinking through how best to harden the temple against a siege, and the group works out how long the northern and southern scouting missions might take. When Alesh arrives with two companions, they agree to regroup in roughly three weeks if they can, discuss Havdar, Tokra, and the emptying villages to the south, and then watch her ride off into the desert before the festival begins.

location.context: markets, caravanserai, and the town edge where Alesh departs
location.notes: The beat stays within town but shifts from shopping and planning to a final farewell scene.

npcs.name: Alesh
npcs.notes: She works out a fallback meeting plan with the party before setting out south that evening.

npcs.name: Havdar
npcs.notes: The party and Alesh treat him as the best person to trust with word left in Tokra if plans go wrong.

npcs.name: Kisa
npcs.notes: She is part of the network of trusted people the party expects to survive the retreat west.

items.name: healing potions
items.notes: The party buys what emergency healing it can before leaving town.

items.name: red healing mud
items.notes: Temple healing supplies remain part of the party's preparation for the northern journey.

facts.shortSummary: On the day before departure, Seeker helps fortify the temple and the party meets Riswynn, a dwarf treasure-hunter also bound for Raven's Hold.
facts.longSummary: The next day is spent in preparation rather than debate. Seeker uses both masonry and magic to strengthen the Temple of Shakun's doors, walls, and approach, leaving Candrosa's people with a much better chance of holding out if the town falls. Back at Ikram's caravanserai, Krom introduces the party to Riswynn Brawnanvil, a dwarf from the Yuvanti Mountains seeking a family heirloom in Raven's Hold, and the group quickly sees that their plans align well enough to travel together.

location.context: the Temple of Shakun and Ikram's caravanserai before the festival
location.notes: The beat moves from temple fortification work to the party's first long conversation with Riswynn.

npcs.name: Krom
npcs.context: innkeeper and matchmaker for the new alliance
npcs.notes: He points Riswynn toward the party after hearing that she also intends to travel to Raven's Hold.

npcs.name: Candrosa
npcs.context: temple leader preparing to stay behind
npcs.notes: The fortification work is explicitly meant to help Candrosa and the remaining mystai survive the coming danger.

items.name: Brawnanvil heirloom
items.notes: Riswynn joins the journey because she hopes to recover a lost family artifact from Raven's Hold.

items.name: Runes of Creation
items.notes: Seeker shows Riswynn the rune-book he was gifted, and she treats it as a striking artifact in its own right.

organizations.name: Shakun Mystai
organizations.notes: The order remains the focus of Seeker's defensive work at the temple.

facts.shortSummary: Karawa's fearful Festival of Rebirth turns from solemn ritual into panic when gnolls and grotesque maw-creatures charge the crowd from the north.
facts.longSummary: The first night of the festival begins with Suma leading the people of Karawa through Jeevali's story of drought, tears, renewal, and the miracle that made Dunmar livable. By the next day's procession the town is still frightened but trying to read hope into every sign, rebuilding the shrine together while preparing to flee west. That fragile calm shatters when a murmur at the edge of the crowd becomes a panic and the party races north to meet a sudden assault by gnolls and monstrous maws.

location.context: the festival grounds and then the northern edge of town near the refugee camp
location.notes: The beat spans the first festival rite, the overnight fast, and the next afternoon's interrupted procession.

npcs.name: Suma
npcs.notes: She interprets the west-drifting embers as a sign that the people should flee west under Jeevali's favor.

items.name: rebirth shrine
items.notes: The burning and rebuilding of the shrine anchors the festival rites before the ambush begins.

mainEnemies.name: gnolls
mainEnemies.notes: Three gnolls lead the attack on the crowd.

mainEnemies.name: maw-creatures
mainEnemies.notes: Three monstrous four-legged mouths charge with the gnolls.

combat.notes: The beat covers the start of the ambush from the first screams through the opening clash.

facts.shortSummary: The battle tightens around the refugee camp as the maws crash into the line, Ander Charmheart stumbles toward them, and Kenzo is mauled nearly to death.
facts.longSummary: Once the fight is fully joined, the maw-creatures prove faster and stranger than the gnolls, bounding across the ground and forcing the party to improvise around walls, elevation, and chaos in the camp. Ander Charmheart emerges in the middle of the attack in a trance-like state, seemingly trying to throw himself to the monsters, and Seeker has to hinder him while also helping hold the line. The fight turns desperate when Kenzo takes a brutal series of bites and collapses to the edge of death just as the party is still trying to understand what these creatures even are.

location.context: the northern camp and improvised fighting lanes between tents and low walls
location.notes: The action stays in the same battle space as the ambush spreads through the camp edge.

npcs.name: Ander Charmheart
npcs.context: halfling caravaner behaving as if drawn toward the maws
npcs.notes: He stumbles toward the battle in a disturbed state and has to be impeded before he reaches the monsters.

mainEnemies.name: gnolls
mainEnemies.notes: They remain part of the melee while the maw-creatures press hardest into the party.

mainEnemies.name: maw-creatures
mainEnemies.notes: Their speed and vicious bites define this phase of the battle.

combat.notes: This beat covers the fight's most dangerous middle stretch, including Ander Charmheart's disturbance and Kenzo's near-fatal injuries.

facts.shortSummary: The party breaks the attackers, leaves one gnoll alive for a moment, and learns from Seeker that the maw-creatures may be extraplanar servants of hunger.
facts.longSummary: The second half of the fight goes decisively in the party's favor as the last maws and gnolls are brought down or subdued. Delwath ties up the sleeping gnoll, but the creature can only snarl and lash out while Ander mirrors the same incoherent bloodlust nearby. As the battlefield settles, Seeker recalls a book on darker planes and identifies the maw-creatures as something not native to this world, turning a local ambush into evidence of a much deeper magical threat.

location.context: the same battle site north of town, now strewn with dead gnolls and fading maw-corpses
location.notes: The fight ends in place before the aftermath spreads back through town.

npcs.name: Ander Charmheart
npcs.context: disturbed halfling witness and would-be victim
npcs.notes: He remains in an altered mental state after the fighting and seems fixated on the maw-creatures.

npcs.name: Havdar
npcs.context: war leader arriving from the other side of the hill
npcs.notes: He reaches the battlefield after dealing with attackers on another front and sees the aftermath.

items.name: Intrusions from the Darker Planes
items.notes: Seeker recalls this old book as the source of his identification of the maw-creatures.

mainEnemies.name: gnolls
mainEnemies.notes: The surviving gnoll is briefly kept alive and bound before the party gives up on meaningful communication.

mainEnemies.name: maw-creatures
mainEnemies.notes: Their corpses rapidly decay or fade as the fight ends.

combat.notes: The beat covers the collapse of organized resistance and the party's first attempt to interpret what they just fought.

facts.shortSummary: In the battle's wake, Karawa abandons any delay: Havdar orders flight at first light, Candrosa and the mystai seal themselves in, and the party turns north at dawn.
facts.longSummary: With initiative over, the group quickly learns that the surviving gnoll cannot be questioned usefully and that the maw-creatures decay with unnatural speed. Havdar arrives bloodied from a parallel skirmish, sees enough to decide the town can no longer wait, and orders the evacuation to begin at first light rather than after another day. Kisa and Candrosa thank the party, the temple is loaded like a siegehold for the mystai who will stay behind, and after a tense night of reflection, packing, and Kenzo's unsettling trance, the town finally pours west toward Tokra while the party picks up the attackers' tracks and rides north toward Raven's Hold.

location.context: the battle's aftermath in and around town, ending with the party taking up the northern trail at dawn
location.notes: The beat begins at the ambush site and resolves with the town's evacuation west and the party's departure north.

npcs.name: Havdar
npcs.context: rear-guard leader forced to accelerate the evacuation
npcs.notes: He confirms he fought other attackers nearby and decides the refugees must leave at first light.

npcs.name: Candrosa
npcs.context: temple leader preparing for a siege
npcs.notes: He and the remaining mystai begin hauling food and water into the Temple of Shakun for a desperate holdout.

npcs.name: Kisa
npcs.notes: She comes to thank the party and acknowledges that the attack has only accelerated the existing plan.

items.name: maw tooth
items.notes: Seeker pries a warm black tooth from one of the creatures as a physical remnant of the fight.

organizations.name: Shakun Mystai
organizations.notes: Candrosa and the remaining mystai prepare to lock themselves into the temple while the rest of Karawa flees west.
